purpose: Continuously turn licensed GDevelop/GDJS templates into approved reusable capability modules, combine them under human-authored Fun Blueprints, and compile deterministic playable projects without fixed archetype boundaries.

semanticReferenceResolution.rule: Every runtime semantic reference resolves by JSON Pointer into the existing semantic dictionary or capability index. Bare labels and a second WP2 semantic dictionary are forbidden.
semanticReferenceResolution.unresolvedDesignRule: A semantic requirement absent from the current dictionary must have exactly one SemanticEngine ownerChangeRequest. It is not runtime-valid until that request is implemented in the existing semantic dictionary and the canonical pointer resolves.

ownerChangeRequests.meaning: Player-controlled movement across both world axes.
Complete a declared finite objective and emit completion evidence.
Aim or select a target and emit a projectile or equivalent attack.
Reach a valid solution state under declared interaction constraints.
Restore a puzzle interaction state to its declared reset state.
Increase a resource-production or progression state through repeated conversion and upgrades.
Convert declared resources into output resources or progression.
Apply a persistent declared upgrade and expose its effect in feedback.

forbidden: use unapproved candidate in online flow
emit final x or y coordinates
copy fixed layouts from archetype ids

owns: semantic and runtime capability cross references

forbidden: run inside online create or continue
embed editor cache or personal data

owns: matching natural play intent to approved blueprint constraints

forbidden: run in online create or continue
let an LLM emit compiler target instructions
declare support from schema presence alone
serve mutable latest without revision pinning

onlineFlow.input: natural request plus safe project context
GameplayRequirementGraph plus FunBlueprintSelection plus ModuleCompositionPlan plus ModuleDeclarationPlan
SpatialCompositionPlan plus ModuleDeclarationPlan plus safe ProjectWorld
tick trace plus semantic requirements plus FunBlueprint assertions

offlineFoundryFlow.action: discover a configured GDevelop/GDJS template source and reject unknown license or missing provenance
emit a content-hashed TemplateSourceRecord pinned to an upstream revision
project project.json, scenes, events, objects, behaviors, variables, resources, extensions, and source spans into deterministic TemplateIR
accept explicit ModuleDebt plus TemplateIR and bind used runtime facts to existing semantic capability ids
separate reusable owned capability structure from project-specific content, configurable parameters, and internal link slots
emit draft ModuleCandidates without direct LLM-authored target instructions
compile candidate create, configure, remove, and replace paths in an isolated fixture workspace
validate artifact, lifecycle, ownership cleanup, runtime behavior, and source trace
prove declared goals, pressures, failure, restart, and reuse outside the source template
promote an immutable revision only when ModulePromotionReceipt passes

invariants: projection is rebuildable from immutable source artifacts
projection supports bidirectional lineage queries
projection never rewrites source artifacts
projection is not approval truth
same ordered receipts produce the same projection hash
unknown or incompatible license is rejected
content hash covers every project file consumed by normalization
source revision is immutable for one record
personal project data is forbidden
same source hash and normalizer version yield the same normalization hash
unsupported structures produce blocking diagnostics instead of silent omission
every normalized node has stable id and source refs
lineage is bidirectional from source through promoted module
loss accounting records consumed, preserved, ignored, and blocking nodes
editor caches and private data are excluded
approved blueprints are immutable and revision pinned
family taxonomy is open and multi-label
blueprint describes play direction rather than a complete project
every mechanic slot resolves through the canonical mechanic registry
playtest assertions are machine measurable
material difference is mechanical or decisional rather than cosmetic, seed-only, or layout-only
human review is required before approval
selection references an approved pinned blueprint
selection does not contain target instructions
all hard blueprint constraints remain visible to ProductModulePlanner
every required semantic ref is satisfied according to exclusive, at-least-one, or composed provider cardinality, or appears in debt
every selected revision is approved and pinned
every operation parameter is declared by the selected manifest
remove and replace prove ownership cleanup and dependent safety
continue uses ProjectWorld modules as base truth
same catalog fingerprint plus same requirement graph plus same base world yields the same plan
declaration is derived only from approved pinned manifests
every spatial role assignment can resolve to a declared subject
bounds and ownership are available before PlacementResolver runs
declaration contains no executable target instruction or final coordinate
topology is selected from module-supported topologies
all required spatial roles are assigned
variation seed changes layout without changing declared game rules
no final coordinate is emitted
all emitted instructions originate from approved manifest templates or deterministic placement emission
no model output is executed as target instructions
ownership receipt lists every created, updated, removed, and retained artifact
continue operations are minimal deltas and do not replay retained module creation
candidate never participates in online composition before a valid promotion receipt inserts an immutable approved revision into the catalog
semantic bindings reference the existing semantic dictionary
TemplateIR source license, source hash, normalization hash, and source spans are recorded
draft manifest exposes no machine instructions to an LLM-facing view
approved revision is immutable
cloud approval requires reusable scope and non-personal provenance
rejection leaves the approved catalog unchanged

semanticContract.rule: All ids reference existing semantic dictionary or capability semantic index entries; no parallel WP2 dictionary is allowed.

spatialContract.rule: Module manifests declare spatial needs and supported topology; they do not own final coordinates.

declarationContract.rule: This is the only approved-manifest source for ModuleDeclarationPlan; declaration code must not mine targetPlan text or invent bounds.

lifecycleContract.rule: Every playable core declares how lifecycle surfaces are provided or required.

ownershipContract.rule: Ownership uses stable artifact ids and explicit shared dependencies sufficient to configure, remove, replace, roll back, and migrate state without guessing.

compilerExtensions.rule: Compiler templates remain internal and deterministic; LLM-facing capabilities never contain target instructions.

acceptanceContract.rule: A module is supported only when its declared semantics and layout variants have executable evidence.

allowedNewArtifacts: source adapter plugin when a genuinely new transport is required
generic normalizer handler plugin when a genuinely new upstream schema or extension node is required
blindIngestionCoverage.passRule: normalize through browser playtest with unchanged protected core hashes
blindIngestionCoverage.unknownStructureRule: fail closed without adding archetype dispatch

recoveryEvidence: same request can resume after catalog or requirement correction

completionEvidence: two independent licensed GDevelop/GDJS template sources pass intake and deterministic TemplateIR replay
unknown license, corrupt template, unsupported extension, and untraceable normalization fail closed
modules extracted from TemplateIR retain source spans and prove reuse outside their source template
new template ingestion requires no ProductModulePlanner or ProductModuleCompiler core dispatch change
approved human-authored Fun Blueprint catalog exists with review receipts
each approved Fun Blueprint has at least two materially distinct browser-playable compositions
Fun Blueprint forbidden combinations and measurable playtest assertions are enforced
all semantic, mechanic, capability, source, blueprint, and module references close through the truth-source DAG
blind pre-audit unseen template reaches browser playtest with unchanged protected core hashes
module conflict and slot fulfillment tests
legacy regression suite remains green without contributing to coverage semantics
layout variation tests proving same rules with distinct topology or seed
offline foundry candidate cannot enter online catalog before promotion

implementationGaps.evidence: The source catalog and rights policy now exist, but no runtime emits immutable TemplateSourceRecords or performs license/hash quarantine.
implementationGaps.closure: At least two independent licensed sources produce immutable TemplateSourceRecords and reject incompatible or unknown licenses.

implementationGaps.evidence: The TemplateIR contract now defines stable nodes, event graph, lineage and loss accounting, but no normalizer implements it for playable project structure.
implementationGaps.closure: GDevelop projects normalize deterministically into TemplateIR with event graph, dependencies, per-node source refs, blocking diagnostics, loss accounting, and replay tests.

implementationGaps.evidence: The canonical mechanic registry exists, but product module manifests and Planner coverage still use legacy capability shapes rather than mechanic-registry refs.
implementationGaps.closure: Blueprint slots, module provides/requires, Foundry annotations, and Planner coverage resolve only through immutable mechanic-registry refs.

implementationGaps.evidence: The open family taxonomy and Blueprint contract exist, but the catalog has no approved human-reviewed Blueprint revisions yet.
implementationGaps.closure: Approved immutable Fun Blueprints express core loop, decisions, pressure, progression, substitutions, forbidden combinations, counterexamples, boundary parameters, and measurable playtest assertions without runtime implementation.

implementationGaps.evidence: Planner selects only by semantic coverage and cannot preserve a human-authored fun direction.
implementationGaps.closure: Online planning consumes a pinned FunBlueprintSelection, enforces capability slots and forbidden combinations, and proves at least two distinct compositions per blueprint.

implementationGaps.evidence: Current high-level dictionary covers platformer-oriented play goals and must receive the ownerChangeRequests before affected fixtures are online-valid.
implementationGaps.closure: Every acceptance semanticRef resolves with the canonical resolver and semantic dictionary checks pass.

implementationGaps.evidence: BuildContract is created before ProductModulePlanner artifacts exist.
implementationGaps.closure: BuildContract remains immutable and carries only GameplayRequirementGraph or its content hash; planner, declaration, spatial, placement, and compiled plans are separate ProjectRun artifacts with their own owners.

implementationGaps.evidence: Current placement context mined bounds from compiler target templates.
implementationGaps.closure: Approved manifests produce ModuleDeclarationPlan before spatial composition and placement; target template mining is deleted.

implementationGaps.evidence: BuildContract ModuleIntent declares remove while module-compiler resolves only install and configure.
implementationGaps.closure: Remove and replace use guarded atomic operations, ownership receipts, shared policies, state migration, rollback tests, and no external patch path.

implementationGaps.evidence: Current online runtime executed intent.bridgePlan.targetPlanText directly.
implementationGaps.closure: Project Weave accepts online module instructions only from hash-verified CompiledModulePlan and rejects or removes legacy bridgePlan target execution.

terraImplementationOrder: T1 preserve the historical five-archetype matrix as regression evidence and remove every claim that it defines template coverage or WP2 completion
T2 define TemplateSourceRecord, license policy, configured template source catalog, source hashing, and upstream revision pinning
T3 implement deterministic TemplateNormalizer for GDevelop project structure, event graphs, external events, resources, extensions, diagnostics, and source spans
T4 define the human-reviewed immutable FunBlueprint catalog and measurable core-loop, decision, pressure, progression, substitution, forbidden-combination, and playtest contracts
T5 implement FunBlueprintSelector and carry pinned blueprint constraints into ModuleCompositionPlan without exposing module implementation to the model
T6 upgrade ProductModuleFoundry to consume TemplateIR, split reusable capability modules from project-specific content, and retain provenance and source traces
T7 prove candidate modules compile and play outside their source templates before immutable ModuleRepository promotion
T8 enforce blueprint capability slots, substitutions, forbidden combinations, and deterministic module selection in ProductModulePlanner
T9 complete guarded remove and replace, ownership cleanup, state migration, and rollback without legacy target-plan bypass
T10 prove one new licensed template enters the catalog without ProductModulePlanner or ProductModuleCompiler core changes
T11 prove every approved Fun Blueprint produces at least two materially distinct browser-playable compositions
T12 wire intake, normalization, foundry, blueprint, composition, regression, failure/recovery, browser, independent testing, and independent audit into the project gate
T13 only after every completionEvidence item exists change WP2 status from designed to implemented

solHandoff.stopCondition: Terra stops and routes back to Sol if template provenance or license is unknown, TemplateIR cannot represent an event dependency without silent loss, a Fun Blueprint needs GDJS instructions or a complete project copy, a new template requires a Planner or Compiler archetype branch, an implementation requires a second semantic dictionary, a model emits target instructions, Foundry runs online, the legacy bridgePlan target path survives, or guarded cleanup and rollback cannot be proved.
